constraints.note: All skeletons are slot-level. They reference component types and PortRole IDs; they do not instantiate concrete component instances.

skeletons.title: Valve-controlled, Load Independent (ConstantPump + 4/3 DCV + Double-Acting Cylinder)

connections.note: Relief valve taps the main pressure line

notes: DCV center condition intended as open-center for load-independent supply.
Skeleton is slot-level: component instances are selected later based on sizing inputs and AAS search.
Pressure concept: load independent.

skeletons.title: Pump-controlled, Open Circuit (Control of rotational speed + ConstantPump + 4/3 DCV + Double-Acting Cylinder)

connections.note: Relief valve taps the main pressure line

notes: DCV center condition intended as closed-center to allow pump control in neutral (conceptual).
Skeleton is slot-level: component instances are selected later based on sizing inputs and AAS search.
Control strategy: rotational_speed.
Motion control is assumed to be achieved primarily via pump/prime-mover control; the DCV is used for direction/neutral handling.

skeletons.title: Pump-controlled, Closed Circuit (Control of rotational speed + Closed-circuit variable pump + Double-Acting Cylinder)

connections.note: Relief taps line A
Relief discharges into opposite line B
Relief taps line B
Relief discharges into opposite line A
Volume compensation / energy storage on loop B (rod-side line)

notes: Closed-circuit pump-controlled cylinder drive: Pump(A/B) ↔ Cylinder(A/B). No tank is shown at system boundary.
Cross-port relief valves protect the main loop against pressure peaks.
Control strategy: rotational_speed.

skeletons.title: Pump-controlled, Closed Circuit (rotational speed control + reversible pump + 3/2 DCV + single-acting plunger + charge/make-up)

componentSlots.name: 3/2 Directional Control Valve (connects cylinder to loop A or B)

connections.note: Loop line A is connected to DCV pressure port (P).
Loop line B is connected to DCV return port (T) (low side when extending).
Single-acting plunger chamber connection.
Relief taps line A
Relief discharges into opposite line B
Relief taps line B
Relief discharges into opposite line A
Charge supply from tank suction
Charge supply from tank suction
Make-up flow into loop A when pressure drops
Make-up flow into loop B when pressure drops
Overpressure relief from loop A to tank
Overpressure relief from loop B to tank

notes: Closed-circuit, pump-controlled single-acting cylinder drive using a 3/2 DCV: the plunger chamber is connected to loop A (pressurize) or loop B (depressurize) instead of tank.
Charge/make-up is represented by check valves from the reservoir to both loop lines. This is necessary because the single-acting chamber introduces volume imbalance in the loop.
Two relief paths are included: cross-port reliefs protect the loop; loop-to-tank reliefs handle volume return and low-side overpressure during retraction/venting.
Control strategy: rotational_speed.
Plunger cylinder is single-acting by definition in this project; therefore 3/2 directional switching is mandatory.

Make-up flow into loop B when pressure drops
